regions.text: When a man is running, his heart is working much harder than when he is sitting down. His blood is being pumped more quickly through his body.
The blood must carry a greater supply of oxygen to the muscles all over the body. Our hearts work for us automatically. A man’s heart can pump as much as three thousand gallons of blood a day.
The amount pumped at any time is controlled automatically. After running hard a man breathes very quickly. He is 'out of breath,' as we say.
running: going faster than walking. automatically: in an automatic way. FUTURE: will run. PRESENT: run(s). PAST: ran, run.
